subject: Human Resources
title: The Cost of a Bad Hire
description: A quick, practical guide to The Cost of a Bad Hire.

hook.text: A bad hire costs 30% of that employee's first-year salary. For a $100K role, that's $30,000—gone.

buildup.text: Costs include recruiting, training, lost productivity, team disruption, and starting the search all over again.

discovery.text: The U.S. Department of Labor estimates bad hires cost companies billions annually across all industries.

twist.text: The hidden cost is morale—good employees lose faith when they see bad hires tolerated or repeated.

climax.text: Investing more time and rigor upfront in hiring saves exponentially more money than fixing mistakes later.

punchline.text: Hire slow, fire fast. The math always favors patience.

quiz.question: What is an often-overlooked cost of a bad hire?
options: Damage to team morale and trust
